titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to George Fletcher, 11 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary to the lord lieutenant of Ireland, on behalf of Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1973-1939), lord lieutenant of Ireland. The letter sent to George Fletcher (c.1863-1934), assistant secretary in respect of technical instruction at the Department of Agriculture. In the letter O'Farrell states that he received Fletcher's previous letter and enclosures and that there does not appear to be anything preventing the examinations referred to from being set on any date arranged by the department.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7686/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
11th May, 1916.
No. 7686. Sir, I am directed by the Lord Lieutenant to
acknowledge the receipt of your letter of the 6th
instant, and enclosure, and in reply to inform you
that there does not appear to be anything in the
present position of affairs to prevent the Technical
School examinations being held on any date now
arranged by the Department. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E O Farrell
G. Fletcher, Esq., Department of Agriculture & Technical
Instruction for Ireland,
Upper Merrion Street,
Dublin.
